steps.title: Define Alvarez's prediction
steps.content: Alvarez argues that debate-driven formats can be less informative than presenting multiple perspectives in a noncompetitive format. The strongest support would show panel viewers becoming better informed than debate viewers.

steps.title: Reject indirect findings
steps.content: Choice A concerns perceived knowledge of commentators, not audience understanding. Choice B also concerns perceived knowledge and does not compare the panel to the debate. Choice D compares the single commentator to the debate, missing the panel.

steps.title: Match information gained
steps.content: The remaining finding shows panel viewers answering more questions correctly than both other groups, directly supporting Alvarez's claim about informative noncompetitive formats. The answer is C.
